Office at Northport and the position
of chief dispatcher will probably fall
to either e. NJ. Flood or j. A. Jor-
dan. The train dispatchers of the
Great Northern in this city have been
handling the S. f. & N. system in ad-
dition to the main line between Troy
and Leavenworth since the two roads
were merged.


j. L. Downey, former chief dis
patcher of the line, has been employ
ed as dispatcher and he would natur-
ally be in line for the position as
chief at Northport had he not declin-
ed to go there. Many of the old
clerks who have been connected with
the S. F. & N. for years will drop out
rather than leave Spokane.


1t is rumored that Mr.. Morgan's
policy will be to operate mixed trains
wherever the passenger and freight
business will not warrant straight
passenger service. This means a re-
duction in the force and that three
or four train crews will be taken off
and some Of the passenger conductors
will be set back on freight trains.
